permissions._comment: Permissions allowlist (inspired by claude-quickstarts autonomous-coding security.py split-validation pattern). Projects extend the allow-list with their own stack-specific commands; the deny-list is the last line of defense even when hooks are bypassed. The Edit/Write path-glob entries (.husky, .github, .claude/*, .githarness) pre-approve paths that Claude Code otherwise treats as sensitive even under --dangerously-skip-permissions, which would otherwise stall a headless agent on an interactive y/n prompt. See https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/settings#permissions for the schema.
permissions._note: allow = auto-approve without prompting the operator. deny = hard-block even when --dangerously-skip-permissions is set (never: wipe home directory, shell-pipe from the network).

PreToolUse.description: Touch .githarness/state/<role>.last-activity on every tool invocation. Watchdog compares this against .last-stop to decide busy vs. idle without scraping the TUI. Fail-open.
Append every tool invocation (pre-phase) to .githarness/audit/<date>.jsonl. Never blocks. Disable via HARNESS_AUDIT_DISABLED=1.
Block --no-verify/--no-gpg-sign on git commit/push so pre-commit, commit-msg, and pre-push hooks cannot be bypassed.

Pre-commit quality check: scan staged files for console.log / debugger / hardcoded secrets (OpenAI / GitHub PAT / AWS keys), validate conventional-commit message format. Blocks commit on error-severity issues; warnings pass through.

Require every agent-authored git commit to carry a `Signed-off-by: <role>@githarness` trailer so `git log` shows which of the three roles authored each commit. All roles share one git identity; the trailer is the only attribution mechanism. No-op for ad-hoc sessions (HARNESS_SESSION_ROLE unset) and for --amend.
Block merges to the release branch (default 'main', override via HARNESS_RELEASE_BRANCH). The release branch is human-only; agents merge to the integration branch (typically 'latest') only.

Require every agent-authored GitHub artifact (issue / PR / comment / review body) to begin with the role badge `[<role> @ <short-id>]`. All three agents share one GitHub identity; this badge is how the operator tells planner from generator from evaluator in the UI. No-op for ad-hoc sessions (HARNESS_SESSION_ROLE unset).

Block `gh issue create|edit` bodies that contain speculative blockers (e.g. '운영자 대기', 'wait for operator') or mis-route work (claim:evaluator on a body describing file-authoring). Such issues deadlock the loop because the claimed agent takes the speculation as fact and never attempts the action. The planner's Branch 5 (self-authored artifact audit) sweeps up anything that slips through.
Block modifications to linter / formatter / TypeScript / Prettier config files so agents cannot silence checks instead of fixing source. Adapted from everything-claude-code under MIT license.

PostToolUse.description: After `gh pr create` completes, surface the PR URL + review command so the operator can jump straight to review. Adapted from everything-claude-code under MIT license.

Warn (non-blocking) on environment-leak patterns in modified files: localhost:PORT, 127.0.0.1, /home/<user>/, 12-digit account IDs, AWS region literals. Skips test/mock/scripts/docs paths. Complements the DoD portability grep by surfacing leaks at edit time.

Stop.description: Touch .githarness/state/<role>.last-stop on turn end. Watchdog uses this plus .last-activity mtime to decide busy vs. idle; 5m-stale + last-activity ahead of last-stop triggers fallback wake, 10m triggers Escape, 15m triggers respawn. Fail-open.
Warn (non-blocking) when modified JS/TS files still contain console.log statements outside test/config/scripts directories. Adapted from everything-claude-code under MIT license.
When a long-lived role session tries to exit, auto-pick up the next issue/PR via scripts/session-next-issue.sh and block /exit with a pickup prompt. No-op when HARNESS_SESSION_ROLE is unset.
